desc: Colombo is Sri Lanka's main international gateway and commercial hub, combining practical transport links with a growing food and café scene. It's an efficient first/last stop for island itineraries, with best west-coast weather in the drier first quarter and more rain risk during inter-monsoon/monsoon phases.
hls: Easy rail and road links onward

todo.name: Pettah market walk
todo.desc: Explore dense market streets, old trading lanes, and local street snacks.

todo.name: Galle Face Green sunset
todo.desc: Join locals for sea breeze, kites, and street food at dusk.

todo.name: National Museum + Independence Square
todo.desc: Cover key colonial and post-independence landmarks in one half day.

todo.name: Café and gallery hop in Colombo 07
todo.desc: Sample contemporary Sri Lankan cafés, boutiques, and creative spaces.

todo.name: Gangaramaya and Seema Malaka
todo.desc: Visit two of Colombo's most recognisable Buddhist sites.

todo.name: South-coast rail day trip
todo.desc: Take a coastal train toward Mount Lavinia, Kalutara, or Bentota.

wifi.note: Strong in business districts and modern hotels; a backup local SIM remains useful for traffic-heavy transfer days.

prac.fltNote: Most UK itineraries use one-stop routings to Colombo (CMB). Return fares are often ~£520–£790 off-peak, ~£790–£1,050 in shoulder demand, and ~£1,050+ around Christmas/New Year and key school-holiday peaks.
prac.lang: Sinhala, Tamil, English common in city areas
prac.visa: Most visitors require an ETA/eVisa before arrival; verify latest Sri Lanka entry rules before booking.
alerts: Airport-to-city transfer time varies widely with traffic (especially weekday peaks).
Heavy rain in wetter months can affect day-trip plans and local transport reliability.
Use registered taxis/ride apps for late arrivals to avoid fare disputes.

sweet: January to March is typically the most reliable weather window for Colombo and Sri Lanka's west coast.
